mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rate compresses equity discount rates and stimulates credit-sensitive spending, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50 percent of observations when FEDFUNDS is below 1 percent.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate rising above 9 percent signals severe labor market slack that crushes aggregate demand expectations and elevates equity risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in 55 percent or more of instances when UNRATE rises above 9 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in industrial production and increases in unemployment jointly signal a collapsing aggregate demand environment that overwhelms isolated data readings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 95.97 and UNRATE at 9.4 as of 2009-07
failure_condition: The interaction term of INDPRO and UNRATE fails to add significant explanatory power beyond main effects in a linear model over any three-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During post-crisis recovery, a month-over-month increase in industrial production signals normalization of factory output and inventory restocking that boosts corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In post-crisis periods, 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive following month-over-month increases in INDPRO, with a t-test p-value exceeding 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in retail sales signals strengthening consumer spending that lifts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50 percent of months following month-over-month RSAFS increases.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year increase in consumer prices above 3 percent triggers fears of impending monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations through higher real discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in 55 percent or more of triggered instances when CPIAUCSL YoY increase exceeds 3 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly increases in housing starts signal a durable bottoming of residential investment that feeds into construction employment and durable goods demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive following two consecutive monthly increases in HOUST, with a t-test p-value exceeding 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls and industrial production confirms a broad-based recovery across labor and supply dimensions that reduces recession probability and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 131488 and INDPRO at 95.97 as of 2009-07
failure_condition: The joint effect of PAYEMS and INDPRO yields a Rank IC below 0.04 over any three-year rolling window.
